description: This figure presents the X-ray diffraction (XRD) characterization of the spark plasma sintered (SPSed) Fe40Mn19Ni15Al15Si10C1 high entropy steel processed by mechanical alloying and SPS at 900 °C and 50 MPa. Panel (a) shows the full XRD pattern spanning 2θ = 30°–100°, identifying the BCC (ferrite) phase as the major constituent, alongside partially ordered B2-type, trace γ-brass type, and Fe5Si3-type intermetallic phases, with characteristic reflections such as BCC (110), B2-type (110), and Fe5Si3-type (210) marked. Panel (b) provides a deconvolution of the overlapped peak region between 51° and 54°, resolving individual contributions from BCC, B2-type, and Fe5Si3-type structures. Panel (c) displays the Rietveld refinement result with observed, calculated, background, and difference profiles, along with Bragg position markers for BCC, B2-type, γ-brass type, and Fe5Si3-type phases. Together, these patterns quantitatively confirm the multiphase nature of the sintered alloy and provide the basis for phase fraction and lattice parameter determination reported in the study.
